title: Adverbs
body: The <alman /> dialect maintains <sd />'s lack of adjectival inflection in adverbial usage, preserving unmodified forms for words functioning as verb or adjective modifiers rather than direct noun descriptors. Two constructions receive special treatment: the adverbial superlative, where the case-marked construction **am** + superlative is replaced by the bare superlative stem, and fixed adverbial expressions of the form preposition + nominalized adjective, whose frozen case endings are replaced by the invariant -e.

rules.title: Adverbs Stay the Same
rules.description:
Words functioning adverbially retain their base form without receiving the -e ending required for attributive adjectives. This applies to:
- Adverbs modifying verbs
- Adjectives modifying other adjectives
- Phrasal modifiers not directly preceding a noun

This rule covers bare adjectives and adverbs. It does not apply to the adverbial superlative construction or to fixed adverbial expressions of the form preposition + nominalized adjective, which are governed by the following rules.

rules.title: Bare-Stem Adverbial Superlatives
rules.description:
The <sd /> adverbial superlative construction **am** + superlative (am besten, am schnellsten) is replaced by the bare superlative stem: **best**, **schnellst**, and so on. This mirrors the English adverbial superlative ("I swim best") and removes the case-marked contraction **am** (an + dem) from the construction entirely, consistent with the elimination of case-marked articles elsewhere in <alman />.

The variant **an** + bare stem (e.g., **an best**) is also acceptable, preserving the prepositional rhythm of the <sd /> construction for speakers who prefer it. The bare stem is the preferred form.

This rule takes precedence over the contraction resolution rule in the section on articles: **am** in adverbial superlatives is not expanded to **an die**. Attributive superlatives are unaffected and follow the regular invariant -e ending rule (die beste Schwimmer).

examples.alman: Ich schwimme best. / Ich schwimme an best.
Er läuft schnellst. / Er läuft an schnellst.
Liebst esse ich Pizza. / An liebst esse ich Pizza.

examples.standard: Dieses Auto gefällt mir am meisten.
examples.alman: Diese Auto gefällt mir meist. / Diese Auto gefällt mir an meist.
examples.english: I like this car the most.

rules.title: Fixed Adverbial Expressions with Nominalized Adjectives
rules.description:
<sd /> contains a family of fixed adverbial expressions built from a preposition and a nominalized adjective carrying a frozen case ending: **unter anderem**, **vor kurzem**, **seit langem**, **bei weitem**, **von neuem**, **ohne weiteres**, and similar variants. In <alman />, these expressions are treated as nominalized adjectives rather than as adverbs: the frozen case ending is replaced by the invariant **-e**, consistent with the rule on nominalized adjectives in the section on adjectives.

This rule takes precedence over the rule that adverbs stay the same: although these expressions function adverbially, their adjective component is inflected with the invariant -e like any other nominalized adjective. Note the contrast with the adverbial superlative: **am** + superlative contains a case-marked article contraction and is replaced by the bare stem, whereas these article-less fixed expressions retain their prepositional structure and take the regular -e ending.
